{{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 10 ({{ill|lt=791|Q31078|WD=Q31078|1=791}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Toyozumi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Toyozumi-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Toyozumi Shrine''' (とよづみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Shimizu Ward|Q79385|WD=Q79385|1=Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, the {{ill|lt=Ninomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}: * '''{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}''' === History === === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=791|Q31078|WD=Q31078|1=791}} ({{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 10){{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} prayed here for victory during his eastern campaign and, on his return, dedicated kagura in celebration; the Yui Otaiko Festival (prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property) is said to originate from that kaguraLocal explanatory signboard.. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho|1=Enchō}} 5), "Toyozumi Shrine" is listed under Ihara District ({{ill|lt=Ibara District|Q988501|WD=Q988501|1=Ihara District, Shizuoka}}), {{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}}. This shrine is widely regarded as that listing{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}For the Engishiki shrine "Toyozumi Shrine," another theory identifies it with Toyoyuke Shrine in Ihara-cho, Shimizu Ward, Shizuoka.. In ''{{ill|lt=Suruga no Kuni Fudoki|Q11668491|WD=Q11668491|1=Suruga no Kuni Fudoki}}'', the entry states "Toyozumi Shrine (or Toyoyuke Shrine), place where {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} was worshipped, the Ninomiya of the province," from which it is inferred that this shrine held second rank after {{ill|lt=Fujisan Hongu Sengen Taisha|Q653180|WD=Q653180|1=Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha}} (the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}) in Suruga{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}}. However, the basis for that statement in the same text is unclear{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, its stamped-land allotment was over 16 koku{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. As seen in documents from {{ill|lt=1589|Q6759|WD=Q6759|1=1589}} ({{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 17), in the early modern period it was also called "Sengen"{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. A petition from {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 21) calls it "Toyozumi Sengen-sha" and claims descent from the Engishiki shrine "Toyozumi Shrine"{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in March {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. === Divine rank === * Junior Fifth Rank Lower (in ''Suruga no Kuni Shinmeicho''){{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}} - written as "Toyozumi Tenjin." === Precincts === The main hall is a fully zelkova-built {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with copper-sheet roofing. Ridge tags state it was built in {{ill|lt=1825|Q6997|WD=Q6997|1=1825}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 8), and before that there were constructions in {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 5) and rebuilding in {{ill|lt=1732|Q6966|WD=Q6966|1=1732}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 17). The roof was originally {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} but was changed to copper sheet in Meiji 22 ({{ill|lt=1889|Q7831|WD=Q7831|1=1889}}). After major repairs in Showa 28 ({{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|WD=Q18585|1=1953}}), it reached its current form. Large {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} and {{ill|lt=camphor|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} trees also stand in the grounds. File:Toyozumi-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Toyozumi-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall === Subsidiary shrines === Precinct shrines are as follows: * Shirahige Shrine - deity: Sarutahiko deity * Five-shrine group ** Yamamiya Shrine - deity: Oyamatsumi deity ** Hie Shrine - deities: Oyamakui-no-Mikoto, Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto ** Suga Shrine - deity: Takesusanoo-no-Mikoto ** Inari Shrine - deity: Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto ** Isomae Shrine - deities: Okuninushi-no-Mikoto, Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto * One-shrine group File:Toyozumi-jinja kusunoki.JPG|Shirahige Shrine with camphor tree behind File:Toyozumi-jinja massha-2.JPG|Five-shrine merged shrine File:Toyozumi-jinja massha.JPG|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yui Otaiko Festival - designated November 17, 1998. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Toyozumi-jinja}} * Local explanatory signboard * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Compiled by Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|year=1912|chapter=Toyozumi Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/908}} "Meiji Jinja Shiryo: Prefectural and District Shrines, Upper"] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frames 908-909. * {{Cite book|和書|year=2000|chapter=Toyozumi Shrine|title=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 22: Place Names of Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Chusei Shokoku Ichinomiya-sei Kenkyukai|year=2000|title=Basic Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System|publisher=Iwata Shoin|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|中世諸国一宮制|2000年}}}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とよつみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:清水区の歴史]] [[Category:清水区の建築物]] [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:駿河国|社2]] [[Category:二宮]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Shrine|WD=Q135039631|1=Toyotsumino Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県静岡市清水区由比町屋原185', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '静岡県静岡市清水区由比町屋原', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Toyozumi-jinja}} [[JA:豊積神社]] {{wikidata link|Q18337536}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Toyozumi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Toyozumi-jinja keidai.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (left) and large ginkgo (right) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}{{ill|lt=Shimizu Ward|Q79385|WD=Q79385|1=Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka}}Yui Machiyahara 185 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 10 ({{ill|lt=791|Q31078|WD=Q31078|1=791}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Toyozumi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Toyozumi-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Toyozumi Shrine''' (とよづみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Shimizu Ward|Q79385|WD=Q79385|1=Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, the {{ill|lt=Ninomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}: * '''{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}''' === History === === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=791|Q31078|WD=Q31078|1=791}} ({{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 10){{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} prayed here for victory during his eastern campaign and, on his return, dedicated kagura in celebration; the Yui Otaiko Festival (prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property) is said to originate from that kaguraLocal explanatory signboard.. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho|1=Enchō}} 5), "Toyozumi Shrine" is listed under Ihara District ({{ill|lt=Ibara District|Q988501|WD=Q988501|1=Ihara District, Shizuoka}}), {{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}}. This shrine is widely regarded as that listing{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}For the Engishiki shrine "Toyozumi Shrine," another theory identifies it with Toyoyuke Shrine in Ihara-cho, Shimizu Ward, Shizuoka.. In ''{{ill|lt=Suruga no Kuni Fudoki|Q11668491|WD=Q11668491|1=Suruga no Kuni Fudoki}}'', the entry states "Toyozumi Shrine (or Toyoyuke Shrine), place where {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} was worshipped, the Ninomiya of the province," from which it is inferred that this shrine held second rank after {{ill|lt=Fujisan Hongu Sengen Taisha|Q653180|WD=Q653180|1=Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha}} (the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}) in Suruga{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}}. However, the basis for that statement in the same text is unclear{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, its stamped-land allotment was over 16 koku{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. As seen in documents from {{ill|lt=1589|Q6759|WD=Q6759|1=1589}} ({{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 17), in the early modern period it was also called "Sengen"{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. A petition from {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 21) calls it "Toyozumi Sengen-sha" and claims descent from the Engishiki shrine "Toyozumi Shrine"{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in March {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. === Divine rank === * Junior Fifth Rank Lower (in ''Suruga no Kuni Shinmeicho''){{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}} - written as "Toyozumi Tenjin." === Precincts === The main hall is a fully zelkova-built {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with copper-sheet roofing. Ridge tags state it was built in {{ill|lt=1825|Q6997|WD=Q6997|1=1825}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 8), and before that there were constructions in {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 5) and rebuilding in {{ill|lt=1732|Q6966|WD=Q6966|1=1732}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 17). The roof was originally {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} but was changed to copper sheet in Meiji 22 ({{ill|lt=1889|Q7831|WD=Q7831|1=1889}}). After major repairs in Showa 28 ({{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|WD=Q18585|1=1953}}), it reached its current form. Large {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} and {{ill|lt=camphor|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} trees also stand in the grounds. File:Toyozumi-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Toyozumi-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall === Subsidiary shrines === Precinct shrines are as follows: * Shirahige Shrine - deity: Sarutahiko deity * Five-shrine group ** Yamamiya Shrine - deity: Oyamatsumi deity ** Hie Shrine - deities: Oyamakui-no-Mikoto, Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto ** Suga Shrine - deity: Takesusanoo-no-Mikoto ** Inari Shrine - deity: Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto ** Isomae Shrine - deities: Okuninushi-no-Mikoto, Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto * One-shrine group File:Toyozumi-jinja kusunoki.JPG|Shirahige Shrine with camphor tree behind File:Toyozumi-jinja massha-2.JPG|Five-shrine merged shrine File:Toyozumi-jinja massha.JPG|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yui Otaiko Festival - designated November 17, 1998. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Toyozumi-jinja}} * Local explanatory signboard * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Compiled by Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|year=1912|chapter=Toyozumi Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/908}} "Meiji Jinja Shiryo: Prefectural and District Shrines, Upper"] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frames 908-909. * {{Cite book|和書|year=2000|chapter=Toyozumi Shrine|title=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 22: Place Names of Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Chusei Shokoku Ichinomiya-sei Kenkyukai|year=2000|title=Basic Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System|publisher=Iwata Shoin|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|中世諸国一宮制|2000年}}}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とよつみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:清水区の歴史]] [[Category:清水区の建築物]] [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:駿河国|社2]] [[Category:二宮]][[Category:qq]]

Shrine|WD=Q135039631|1=Toyotsumino Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県静岡市清水区由比町屋原185', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '静岡県静岡市清水区由比町屋原', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Toyozumi-jinja}} [[JA:豊積神社]] {{wikidata link|Q18337536}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Toyozumi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Toyozumi-jinja keidai.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (left) and large ginkgo (right) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}{{ill|lt=Shimizu Ward|Q79385|WD=Q79385|1=Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka}}Yui Machiyahara 185 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 10 ({{ill|lt=791|Q31078|WD=Q31078|1=791}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Toyozumi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Toyozumi-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Toyozumi Shrine''' (とよづみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Shimizu Ward|Q79385|WD=Q79385|1=Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, the {{ill|lt=Ninomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}: * '''{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}''' === History === === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=791|Q31078|WD=Q31078|1=791}} ({{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 10){{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} prayed here for victory during his eastern campaign and, on his return, dedicated kagura in celebration; the Yui Otaiko Festival (prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property) is said to originate from that kaguraLocal explanatory signboard.. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho|1=Enchō}} 5), "Toyozumi Shrine" is listed under Ihara District ({{ill|lt=Ibara District|Q988501|WD=Q988501|1=Ihara District, Shizuoka}}), {{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}}. This shrine is widely regarded as that listing{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}For the Engishiki shrine "Toyozumi Shrine," another theory identifies it with Toyoyuke Shrine in Ihara-cho, Shimizu Ward, Shizuoka.. In ''{{ill|lt=Suruga no Kuni Fudoki|Q11668491|WD=Q11668491|1=Suruga no Kuni Fudoki}}'', the entry states "Toyozumi Shrine (or Toyoyuke Shrine), place where {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} was worshipped, the Ninomiya of the province," from which it is inferred that this shrine held second rank after {{ill|lt=Fujisan Hongu Sengen Taisha|Q653180|WD=Q653180|1=Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha}} (the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}) in Suruga{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}}. However, the basis for that statement in the same text is unclear{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, its stamped-land allotment was over 16 koku{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. As seen in documents from {{ill|lt=1589|Q6759|WD=Q6759|1=1589}} ({{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 17), in the early modern period it was also called "Sengen"{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. A petition from {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 21) calls it "Toyozumi Sengen-sha" and claims descent from the Engishiki shrine "Toyozumi Shrine"{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in March {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. === Divine rank === * Junior Fifth Rank Lower (in ''Suruga no Kuni Shinmeicho''){{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}} - written as "Toyozumi Tenjin." === Precincts === The main hall is a fully zelkova-built {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with copper-sheet roofing. Ridge tags state it was built in {{ill|lt=1825|Q6997|WD=Q6997|1=1825}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 8), and before that there were constructions in {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 5) and rebuilding in {{ill|lt=1732|Q6966|WD=Q6966|1=1732}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 17). The roof was originally {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} but was changed to copper sheet in Meiji 22 ({{ill|lt=1889|Q7831|WD=Q7831|1=1889}}). After major repairs in Showa 28 ({{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|WD=Q18585|1=1953}}), it reached its current form. Large {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} and {{ill|lt=camphor|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} trees also stand in the grounds. File:Toyozumi-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Toyozumi-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall === Subsidiary shrines === Precinct shrines are as follows: * Shirahige Shrine - deity: Sarutahiko deity * Five-shrine group ** Yamamiya Shrine - deity: Oyamatsumi deity ** Hie Shrine - deities: Oyamakui-no-Mikoto, Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto ** Suga Shrine - deity: Takesusanoo-no-Mikoto ** Inari Shrine - deity: Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto ** Isomae Shrine - deities: Okuninushi-no-Mikoto, Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto * One-shrine group File:Toyozumi-jinja kusunoki.JPG|Shirahige Shrine with camphor tree behind File:Toyozumi-jinja massha-2.JPG|Five-shrine merged shrine File:Toyozumi-jinja massha.JPG|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yui Otaiko Festival - designated November 17, 1998. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Toyozumi-jinja}} * Local explanatory signboard * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Compiled by Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|year=1912|chapter=Toyozumi Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/908}} "Meiji Jinja Shiryo: Prefectural and District Shrines, Upper"] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frames 908-909. * {{Cite book|和書|year=2000|chapter=Toyozumi Shrine|title=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 22: Place Names of Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Chusei Shokoku Ichinomiya-sei Kenkyukai|year=2000|title=Basic Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System|publisher=Iwata Shoin|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|中世諸国一宮制|2000年}}}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:清水区の歴史]] [[Category:清水区の建築物]] [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:駿河国|社2]] [[Category:二宮]][[Category:qq]]

Shrine|WD=Q135039631|1=Toyotsumino Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県静岡市清水区由比町屋原185', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '静岡県静岡市清水区由比町屋原', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Toyozumi-jinja}} [[JA:豊積神社]] {{wikidata link|Q18337536}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Toyozumi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Toyozumi-jinja keidai.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (left) and large ginkgo (right) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}{{ill|lt=Shimizu Ward|Q79385|WD=Q79385|1=Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka}}Yui Machiyahara 185 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 10 ({{ill|lt=791|Q31078|WD=Q31078|1=791}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Toyozumi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Toyozumi-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Toyozumi Shrine''' (とよづみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Shimizu Ward|Q79385|WD=Q79385|1=Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, the {{ill|lt=Ninomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}: * '''{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}''' === History === === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=791|Q31078|WD=Q31078|1=791}} ({{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 10){{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} prayed here for victory during his eastern campaign and, on his return, dedicated kagura in celebration; the Yui Otaiko Festival (prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property) is said to originate from that kaguraLocal explanatory signboard.. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho|1=Enchō}} 5), "Toyozumi Shrine" is listed under Ihara District ({{ill|lt=Ibara District|Q988501|WD=Q988501|1=Ihara District, Shizuoka}}), {{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}}. This shrine is widely regarded as that listing{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}For the Engishiki shrine "Toyozumi Shrine," another theory identifies it with Toyoyuke Shrine in Ihara-cho, Shimizu Ward, Shizuoka.. In ''{{ill|lt=Suruga no Kuni Fudoki|Q11668491|WD=Q11668491|1=Suruga no Kuni Fudoki}}'', the entry states "Toyozumi Shrine (or Toyoyuke Shrine), place where {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} was worshipped, the Ninomiya of the province," from which it is inferred that this shrine held second rank after {{ill|lt=Fujisan Hongu Sengen Taisha|Q653180|WD=Q653180|1=Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha}} (the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}) in Suruga{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}}. However, the basis for that statement in the same text is unclear{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, its stamped-land allotment was over 16 koku{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. As seen in documents from {{ill|lt=1589|Q6759|WD=Q6759|1=1589}} ({{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 17), in the early modern period it was also called "Sengen"{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. A petition from {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 21) calls it "Toyozumi Sengen-sha" and claims descent from the Engishiki shrine "Toyozumi Shrine"{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in March {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. === Divine rank === * Junior Fifth Rank Lower (in ''Suruga no Kuni Shinmeicho''){{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}} - written as "Toyozumi Tenjin." === Precincts === The main hall is a fully zelkova-built {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with copper-sheet roofing. Ridge tags state it was built in {{ill|lt=1825|Q6997|WD=Q6997|1=1825}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 8), and before that there were constructions in {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 5) and rebuilding in {{ill|lt=1732|Q6966|WD=Q6966|1=1732}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 17). The roof was originally {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} but was changed to copper sheet in Meiji 22 ({{ill|lt=1889|Q7831|WD=Q7831|1=1889}}). After major repairs in Showa 28 ({{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|WD=Q18585|1=1953}}), it reached its current form. Large {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} and {{ill|lt=camphor|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} trees also stand in the grounds. File:Toyozumi-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Toyozumi-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall === Subsidiary shrines === Precinct shrines are as follows: * Shirahige Shrine - deity: Sarutahiko deity * Five-shrine group ** Yamamiya Shrine - deity: Oyamatsumi deity ** Hie Shrine - deities: Oyamakui-no-Mikoto, Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto ** Suga Shrine - deity: Takesusanoo-no-Mikoto ** Inari Shrine - deity: Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto ** Isomae Shrine - deities: Okuninushi-no-Mikoto, Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto * One-shrine group File:Toyozumi-jinja kusunoki.JPG|Shirahige Shrine with camphor tree behind File:Toyozumi-jinja massha-2.JPG|Five-shrine merged shrine File:Toyozumi-jinja massha.JPG|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yui Otaiko Festival - designated November 17, 1998. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Toyozumi-jinja}} * Local explanatory signboard * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Compiled by Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|year=1912|chapter=Toyozumi Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/908}} "Meiji Jinja Shiryo: Prefectural and District Shrines, Upper"] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frames 908-909. * {{Cite book|和書|year=2000|chapter=Toyozumi Shrine|title=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 22: Place Names of Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Chusei Shokoku Ichinomiya-sei Kenkyukai|year=2000|title=Basic Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System|publisher=Iwata Shoin|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|中世諸国一宮制|2000年}}}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:清水区の歴史]] [[Category:清水区の建築物]] [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:駿河国|社2]] [[Category:二宮]]

{{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 10 ({{ill|lt=791|Q31078|WD=Q31078|1=791}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Toyozumi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Toyozumi-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Toyozumi Shrine''' (とよづみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Shimizu Ward|Q79385|WD=Q79385|1=Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, the {{ill|lt=Ninomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}: * '''{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}''' === History === === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=791|Q31078|WD=Q31078|1=791}} ({{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 10){{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} prayed here for victory during his eastern campaign and, on his return, dedicated kagura in celebration; the Yui Otaiko Festival (prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property) is said to originate from that kaguraLocal explanatory signboard.. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho|1=Enchō}} 5), "Toyozumi Shrine" is listed under Ihara District ({{ill|lt=Ibara District|Q988501|WD=Q988501|1=Ihara District, Shizuoka}}), {{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}}. This shrine is widely regarded as that listing{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}For the Engishiki shrine "Toyozumi Shrine," another theory identifies it with Toyoyuke Shrine in Ihara-cho, Shimizu Ward, Shizuoka.. In ''{{ill|lt=Suruga no Kuni Fudoki|Q11668491|WD=Q11668491|1=Suruga no Kuni Fudoki}}'', the entry states "Toyozumi Shrine (or Toyoyuke Shrine), place where {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} was worshipped, the Ninomiya of the province," from which it is inferred that this shrine held second rank after {{ill|lt=Fujisan Hongu Sengen Taisha|Q653180|WD=Q653180|1=Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha}} (the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}) in Suruga{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}}. However, the basis for that statement in the same text is unclear{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, its stamped-land allotment was over 16 koku{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. As seen in documents from {{ill|lt=1589|Q6759|WD=Q6759|1=1589}} ({{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 17), in the early modern period it was also called "Sengen"{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. A petition from {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 21) calls it "Toyozumi Sengen-sha" and claims descent from the Engishiki shrine "Toyozumi Shrine"{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in March {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. === Divine rank === * Junior Fifth Rank Lower (in ''Suruga no Kuni Shinmeicho''){{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}} - written as "Toyozumi Tenjin." === Precincts === The main hall is a fully zelkova-built {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with copper-sheet roofing. Ridge tags state it was built in {{ill|lt=1825|Q6997|WD=Q6997|1=1825}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 8), and before that there were constructions in {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 5) and rebuilding in {{ill|lt=1732|Q6966|WD=Q6966|1=1732}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 17). The roof was originally {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} but was changed to copper sheet in Meiji 22 ({{ill|lt=1889|Q7831|WD=Q7831|1=1889}}). After major repairs in Showa 28 ({{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|WD=Q18585|1=1953}}), it reached its current form. Large {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} and {{ill|lt=camphor|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} trees also stand in the grounds. File:Toyozumi-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Toyozumi-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall === Subsidiary shrines === Precinct shrines are as follows: * Shirahige Shrine - deity: Sarutahiko deity * Five-shrine group ** Yamamiya Shrine - deity: Oyamatsumi deity ** Hie Shrine - deities: Oyamakui-no-Mikoto, Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto ** Suga Shrine - deity: Takesusanoo-no-Mikoto ** Inari Shrine - deity: Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto ** Isomae Shrine - deities: Okuninushi-no-Mikoto, Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto * One-shrine group File:Toyozumi-jinja kusunoki.JPG|Shirahige Shrine with camphor tree behind File:Toyozumi-jinja massha-2.JPG|Five-shrine merged shrine File:Toyozumi-jinja massha.JPG|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yui Otaiko Festival - designated November 17, 1998. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Toyozumi-jinja}} * Local explanatory signboard * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Compiled by Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|year=1912|chapter=Toyozumi Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/908}} "Meiji Jinja Shiryo: Prefectural and District Shrines, Upper"] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frames 908-909. * {{Cite book|和書|year=2000|chapter=Toyozumi Shrine|title=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 22: Place Names of Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Chusei Shokoku Ichinomiya-sei Kenkyukai|year=2000|title=Basic Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System|publisher=Iwata Shoin|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|中世諸国一宮制|2000年}}}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:清水区の歴史]] [[Category:清水区の建築物]] [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:駿河国|社2]] [[Category:Ninomiya]]

Shrine|WD=Q135039631|1=Toyotsumino Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県静岡市清水区由比町屋原185', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '静岡県静岡市清水区由比町屋原', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Toyozumi-jinja}} [[JA:豊積神社]] {{wikidata link|Q18337536}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Toyozumi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Toyozumi-jinja keidai.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (left) and large ginkgo (right) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}{{ill|lt=Shimizu Ward|Q79385|WD=Q79385|1=Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka}}Yui Machiyahara 185 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 10 ({{ill|lt=791|Q31078|WD=Q31078|1=791}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Toyozumi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Toyozumi-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Toyozumi Shrine''' (とよづみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Shimizu Ward|Q79385|WD=Q79385|1=Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, the {{ill|lt=Ninomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}: * '''{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}''' === History === === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=791|Q31078|WD=Q31078|1=791}} ({{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 10){{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} prayed here for victory during his eastern campaign and, on his return, dedicated kagura in celebration; the Yui Otaiko Festival (prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property) is said to originate from that kaguraLocal explanatory signboard.. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho|1=Enchō}} 5), "Toyozumi Shrine" is listed under Ihara District ({{ill|lt=Ibara District|Q988501|WD=Q988501|1=Ihara District, Shizuoka}}), {{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}}. This shrine is widely regarded as that listing{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}For the Engishiki shrine "Toyozumi Shrine," another theory identifies it with Toyoyuke Shrine in Ihara-cho, Shimizu Ward, Shizuoka.. In ''{{ill|lt=Suruga no Kuni Fudoki|Q11668491|WD=Q11668491|1=Suruga no Kuni Fudoki}}'', the entry states "Toyozumi Shrine (or Toyoyuke Shrine), place where {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} was worshipped, the Ninomiya of the province," from which it is inferred that this shrine held second rank after {{ill|lt=Fujisan Hongu Sengen Taisha|Q653180|WD=Q653180|1=Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha}} (the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}) in Suruga{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}}. However, the basis for that statement in the same text is unclear{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, its stamped-land allotment was over 16 koku{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. As seen in documents from {{ill|lt=1589|Q6759|WD=Q6759|1=1589}} ({{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 17), in the early modern period it was also called "Sengen"{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. A petition from {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 21) calls it "Toyozumi Sengen-sha" and claims descent from the Engishiki shrine "Toyozumi Shrine"{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in March {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. === Divine rank === * Junior Fifth Rank Lower (in ''Suruga no Kuni Shinmeicho''){{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}} - written as "Toyozumi Tenjin." === Precincts === The main hall is a fully zelkova-built {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with copper-sheet roofing. Ridge tags state it was built in {{ill|lt=1825|Q6997|WD=Q6997|1=1825}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 8), and before that there were constructions in {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 5) and rebuilding in {{ill|lt=1732|Q6966|WD=Q6966|1=1732}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 17). The roof was originally {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} but was changed to copper sheet in Meiji 22 ({{ill|lt=1889|Q7831|WD=Q7831|1=1889}}). After major repairs in Showa 28 ({{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|WD=Q18585|1=1953}}), it reached its current form. Large {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} and {{ill|lt=camphor|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} trees also stand in the grounds. File:Toyozumi-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Toyozumi-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall === Subsidiary shrines === Precinct shrines are as follows: * Shirahige Shrine - deity: Sarutahiko deity * Five-shrine group ** Yamamiya Shrine - deity: Oyamatsumi deity ** Hie Shrine - deities: Oyamakui-no-Mikoto, Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto ** Suga Shrine - deity: Takesusanoo-no-Mikoto ** Inari Shrine - deity: Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto ** Isomae Shrine - deities: Okuninushi-no-Mikoto, Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto * One-shrine group File:Toyozumi-jinja kusunoki.JPG|Shirahige Shrine with camphor tree behind File:Toyozumi-jinja massha-2.JPG|Five-shrine merged shrine File:Toyozumi-jinja massha.JPG|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yui Otaiko Festival - designated November 17, 1998. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Toyozumi-jinja}} * Local explanatory signboard * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Compiled by Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|year=1912|chapter=Toyozumi Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/908}} "Meiji Jinja Shiryo: Prefectural and District Shrines, Upper"] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frames 908-909. * {{Cite book|和書|year=2000|chapter=Toyozumi Shrine|title=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 22: Place Names of Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Chusei Shokoku Ichinomiya-sei Kenkyukai|year=2000|title=Basic Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System|publisher=Iwata Shoin|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|中世諸国一宮制|2000年}}}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:清水区の歴史]] [[Category:清水区の建築物]] [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:駿河国|社2]] [[Category:Ninomiya]]

Shrine|WD=Q135039631|1=Toyotsumino Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県静岡市清水区由比町屋原185', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '静岡県静岡市清水区由比町屋原', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Toyozumi-jinja}} [[JA:豊積神社]] {{wikidata link|Q18337536}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Toyozumi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Toyozumi-jinja keidai.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (left) and large ginkgo (right) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}{{ill|lt=Shimizu Ward|Q79385|WD=Q79385|1=Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka}}Yui Machiyahara 185 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 10 ({{ill|lt=791|Q31078|WD=Q31078|1=791}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Toyozumi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Toyozumi-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Toyozumi Shrine''' (とよづみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Shimizu Ward|Q79385|WD=Q79385|1=Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, the {{ill|lt=Ninomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}: * '''{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}''' === History === === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=791|Q31078|WD=Q31078|1=791}} ({{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 10){{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} prayed here for victory during his eastern campaign and, on his return, dedicated kagura in celebration; the Yui Otaiko Festival (prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property) is said to originate from that kaguraLocal explanatory signboard.. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho|1=Enchō}} 5), "Toyozumi Shrine" is listed under Ihara District ({{ill|lt=Ibara District|Q988501|WD=Q988501|1=Ihara District, Shizuoka}}), {{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}}. This shrine is widely regarded as that listing{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}For the Engishiki shrine "Toyozumi Shrine," another theory identifies it with Toyoyuke Shrine in Ihara-cho, Shimizu Ward, Shizuoka.. In ''{{ill|lt=Suruga no Kuni Fudoki|Q11668491|WD=Q11668491|1=Suruga no Kuni Fudoki}}'', the entry states "Toyozumi Shrine (or Toyoyuke Shrine), place where {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} was worshipped, the Ninomiya of the province," from which it is inferred that this shrine held second rank after {{ill|lt=Fujisan Hongu Sengen Taisha|Q653180|WD=Q653180|1=Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha}} (the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}) in Suruga{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}}. However, the basis for that statement in the same text is unclear{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, its stamped-land allotment was over 16 koku{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. As seen in documents from {{ill|lt=1589|Q6759|WD=Q6759|1=1589}} ({{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 17), in the early modern period it was also called "Sengen"{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. A petition from {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 21) calls it "Toyozumi Sengen-sha" and claims descent from the Engishiki shrine "Toyozumi Shrine"{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in March {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. === Divine rank === * Junior Fifth Rank Lower (in ''Suruga no Kuni Shinmeicho''){{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}} - written as "Toyozumi Tenjin." === Precincts === The main hall is a fully zelkova-built {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with copper-sheet roofing. Ridge tags state it was built in {{ill|lt=1825|Q6997|WD=Q6997|1=1825}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 8), and before that there were constructions in {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 5) and rebuilding in {{ill|lt=1732|Q6966|WD=Q6966|1=1732}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 17). The roof was originally {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} but was changed to copper sheet in Meiji 22 ({{ill|lt=1889|Q7831|WD=Q7831|1=1889}}). After major repairs in Showa 28 ({{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|WD=Q18585|1=1953}}), it reached its current form. Large {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} and {{ill|lt=camphor|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} trees also stand in the grounds. File:Toyozumi-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Toyozumi-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall === Subsidiary shrines === Precinct shrines are as follows: * Shirahige Shrine - deity: Sarutahiko deity * Five-shrine group ** Yamamiya Shrine - deity: Oyamatsumi deity ** Hie Shrine - deities: Oyamakui-no-Mikoto, Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto ** Suga Shrine - deity: Takesusanoo-no-Mikoto ** Inari Shrine - deity: Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto ** Isomae Shrine - deities: Okuninushi-no-Mikoto, Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto * One-shrine group File:Toyozumi-jinja kusunoki.JPG|Shirahige Shrine with camphor tree behind File:Toyozumi-jinja massha-2.JPG|Five-shrine merged shrine File:Toyozumi-jinja massha.JPG|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yui Otaiko Festival - designated November 17, 1998. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Toyozumi-jinja}} * Local explanatory signboard * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Compiled by Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|year=1912|chapter=Toyozumi Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/908}} "Meiji Jinja Shiryo: Prefectural and District Shrines, Upper"] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frames 908-909. * {{Cite book|和書|year=2000|chapter=Toyozumi Shrine|title=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 22: Place Names of Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Chusei Shokoku Ichinomiya-sei Kenkyukai|year=2000|title=Basic Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System|publisher=Iwata Shoin|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|中世諸国一宮制|2000年}}}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:清水区の歴史]] [[Category:清水区の建築物]] [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:駿河国|社2]] [[Category:Ninomiya]]

Shrine|WD=Q135039631|1=Toyotsumino Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '静岡県静岡市清水区由比町屋原185', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '静岡県静岡市清水区由比町屋原', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Toyozumi-jinja}} [[JA:豊積神社]] {{wikidata link|Q18337536}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Toyozumi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Toyozumi-jinja keidai.JPG|270px]]
Haiden (left) and large ginkgo (right) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}{{ill|lt=Shimizu Ward|Q79385|WD=Q79385|1=Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka}}Yui Machiyahara 185 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
{{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 10 ({{ill|lt=791|Q31078|WD=Q31078|1=791}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Toyozumi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Toyozumi-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Toyozumi Shrine''' (とよづみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Shimizu Ward|Q79385|WD=Q79385|1=Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, the {{ill|lt=Ninomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}: * '''{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}''' === History === === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=791|Q31078|WD=Q31078|1=791}} ({{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 10){{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} prayed here for victory during his eastern campaign and, on his return, dedicated kagura in celebration; the Yui Otaiko Festival (prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property) is said to originate from that kaguraLocal explanatory signboard.. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho|1=Enchō}} 5), "Toyozumi Shrine" is listed under Ihara District ({{ill|lt=Ibara District|Q988501|WD=Q988501|1=Ihara District, Shizuoka}}), {{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}}. This shrine is widely regarded as that listing{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}For the Engishiki shrine "Toyozumi Shrine," another theory identifies it with Toyoyuke Shrine in Ihara-cho, Shimizu Ward, Shizuoka.. In ''{{ill|lt=Suruga no Kuni Fudoki|Q11668491|WD=Q11668491|1=Suruga no Kuni Fudoki}}'', the entry states "Toyozumi Shrine (or Toyoyuke Shrine), place where {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} was worshipped, the Ninomiya of the province," from which it is inferred that this shrine held second rank after {{ill|lt=Fujisan Hongu Sengen Taisha|Q653180|WD=Q653180|1=Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha}} (the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}) in Suruga{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}}. However, the basis for that statement in the same text is unclear{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, its stamped-land allotment was over 16 koku{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. As seen in documents from {{ill|lt=1589|Q6759|WD=Q6759|1=1589}} ({{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 17), in the early modern period it was also called "Sengen"{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. A petition from {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 21) calls it "Toyozumi Sengen-sha" and claims descent from the Engishiki shrine "Toyozumi Shrine"{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in March {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. === Divine rank === * Junior Fifth Rank Lower (in ''Suruga no Kuni Shinmeicho''){{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}} - written as "Toyozumi Tenjin." === Precincts === The main hall is a fully zelkova-built {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with copper-sheet roofing. Ridge tags state it was built in {{ill|lt=1825|Q6997|WD=Q6997|1=1825}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 8), and before that there were constructions in {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 5) and rebuilding in {{ill|lt=1732|Q6966|WD=Q6966|1=1732}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 17). The roof was originally {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} but was changed to copper sheet in Meiji 22 ({{ill|lt=1889|Q7831|WD=Q7831|1=1889}}). After major repairs in Showa 28 ({{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|WD=Q18585|1=1953}}), it reached its current form. Large {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} and {{ill|lt=camphor|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} trees also stand in the grounds. File:Toyozumi-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Toyozumi-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall === Subsidiary shrines === Precinct shrines are as follows: * Shirahige Shrine - deity: Sarutahiko deity * Five-shrine group ** Yamamiya Shrine - deity: Oyamatsumi deity ** Hie Shrine - deities: Oyamakui-no-Mikoto, Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto ** Suga Shrine - deity: Takesusanoo-no-Mikoto ** Inari Shrine - deity: Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto ** Isomae Shrine - deities: Okuninushi-no-Mikoto, Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto * One-shrine group File:Toyozumi-jinja kusunoki.JPG|Shirahige Shrine with camphor tree behind File:Toyozumi-jinja massha-2.JPG|Five-shrine merged shrine File:Toyozumi-jinja massha.JPG|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yui Otaiko Festival - designated November 17, 1998. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Toyozumi-jinja}} * Local explanatory signboard * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Compiled by Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|year=1912|chapter=Toyozumi Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/908}} "Meiji Jinja Shiryo: Prefectural and District Shrines, Upper"] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frames 908-909. * {{Cite book|和書|year=2000|chapter=Toyozumi Shrine|title=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 22: Place Names of Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Chusei Shokoku Ichinomiya-sei Kenkyukai|year=2000|title=Basic Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System|publisher=Iwata Shoin|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|中世諸国一宮制|2000年}}}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:清水区の歴史]] [[Category:清水区の建築物]] [[Category:駿河国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Suruga Province]] [[Category:Ninomiya]]

{{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 10 ({{ill|lt=791|Q31078|WD=Q31078|1=791}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922|1=October 14}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Toyozumi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Toyozumi-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Toyozumi Shrine''' (とよづみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Shimizu Ward|Q79385|WD=Q79385|1=Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka|Q174691|WD=Q174691|1=Shizuoka (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, the {{ill|lt=Ninomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}: * '''{{ill|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}''' === History === === Overview === According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=791|Q31078|WD=Q31078|1=791}} ({{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 10){{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Q941979|WD=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} prayed here for victory during his eastern campaign and, on his return, dedicated kagura in celebration; the Yui Otaiko Festival (prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property) is said to originate from that kaguraLocal explanatory signboard.. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' {{ill|lt=Jinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Encho (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Encho|1=Enchō}} 5), "Toyozumi Shrine" is listed under Ihara District ({{ill|lt=Ibara District|Q988501|WD=Q988501|1=Ihara District, Shizuoka}}), {{ill|lt=Suruga Province|Q864170|WD=Q864170|1=Suruga Province}}. This shrine is widely regarded as that listing{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}For the Engishiki shrine "Toyozumi Shrine," another theory identifies it with Toyoyuke Shrine in Ihara-cho, Shimizu Ward, Shizuoka.. In ''{{ill|lt=Suruga no Kuni Fudoki|Q11668491|WD=Q11668491|1=Suruga no Kuni Fudoki}}'', the entry states "Toyozumi Shrine (or Toyoyuke Shrine), place where {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} was worshipped, the Ninomiya of the province," from which it is inferred that this shrine held second rank after {{ill|lt=Fujisan Hongu Sengen Taisha|Q653180|WD=Q653180|1=Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha}} (the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}) in Suruga{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}}. However, the basis for that statement in the same text is unclear{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, its stamped-land allotment was over 16 koku{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. As seen in documents from {{ill|lt=1589|Q6759|WD=Q6759|1=1589}} ({{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 17), in the early modern period it was also called "Sengen"{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. A petition from {{ill|lt=1736|Q6989|WD=Q6989|1=1736}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 21) calls it "Toyozumi Sengen-sha" and claims descent from the Engishiki shrine "Toyozumi Shrine"{{Sfn|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in March {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} under the modern shrine ranking system{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. === Divine rank === * Junior Fifth Rank Lower (in ''Suruga no Kuni Shinmeicho''){{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=151}} - written as "Toyozumi Tenjin." === Precincts === The main hall is a fully zelkova-built {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with copper-sheet roofing. Ridge tags state it was built in {{ill|lt=1825|Q6997|WD=Q6997|1=1825}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 8), and before that there were constructions in {{ill|lt=1600|Q6720|WD=Q6720|1=1600}} ({{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 5) and rebuilding in {{ill|lt=1732|Q6966|WD=Q6966|1=1732}} ({{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 17). The roof was originally {{ill|lt=cypress bark roofing|Q90373477|WD=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} but was changed to copper sheet in Meiji 22 ({{ill|lt=1889|Q7831|WD=Q7831|1=1889}}). After major repairs in Showa 28 ({{ill|lt=1953|Q18585|WD=Q18585|1=1953}}), it reached its current form. Large {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} and {{ill|lt=camphor|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} trees also stand in the grounds. File:Toyozumi-jinja honden.JPG|Main hall File:Toyozumi-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship hall === Subsidiary shrines === Precinct shrines are as follows: * Shirahige Shrine - deity: Sarutahiko deity * Five-shrine group ** Yamamiya Shrine - deity: Oyamatsumi deity ** Hie Shrine - deities: Oyamakui-no-Mikoto, Hayasusanoo-no-Mikoto ** Suga Shrine - deity: Takesusanoo-no-Mikoto ** Inari Shrine - deity: Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto ** Isomae Shrine - deities: Okuninushi-no-Mikoto, Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto * One-shrine group File:Toyozumi-jinja kusunoki.JPG|Shirahige Shrine with camphor tree behind File:Toyozumi-jinja massha-2.JPG|Five-shrine merged shrine File:Toyozumi-jinja massha.JPG|Precinct shrine === Cultural properties === === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Yui Otaiko Festival - designated November 17, 1998. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''References''' {{reflist}} === Bibliography === {{Commonscat|Toyozumi-jinja}} * Local explanatory signboard * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Compiled by Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|year=1912|chapter=Toyozumi Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gosha Meiji Jinja Shiryo|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryo Hensansho|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/908}} "Meiji Jinja Shiryo: Prefectural and District Shrines, Upper"] (National Diet Library Digital Collection), frames 908-909. * {{Cite book|和書|year=2000|chapter=Toyozumi Shrine|title=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 22: Place Names of Shizuoka Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|豊積神社（平）|2000年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Chusei Shokoku Ichinomiya-sei Kenkyukai|year=2000|title=Basic Study of the Medieval Ichinomiya System|publisher=Iwata Shoin|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|中世諸国一宮制|2000年}}}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Toyozumi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:清水区の歴史]] [[Category:清水区の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Suruga Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Suruga Province]] [[Category:Ninomiya]]
